reasoning: The world record for the women's 100m breaststroke (long course) is 1:04.13, set by Lilly King in July 2017. Wikipedia confirms her as the record holder as of November 13, 2025. Between November and February, competitive swimming overwhelmingly focuses on short course meters (SCM) or short course yards (SCY), not long course meters (LCM). With the 2025 World Aquatics Swimming World Cup and 2025 winter competitions being largely short course, elite swimmers do not typically peak or attempt LCM world records during this window. Historically, zero of the current women's LCM individual world records were set between November and February. Furthermore, King's record has stood for over 8 years, proving highly resilient. The chances of it being broken in an off-season period within the next 3 months are extremely low. I am very confident she will retain the record across all three resolution dates.
